Description: Iraq invades Kuwait leading to international coalition intervention and severe crude supply anxieties.
Financial contagion triggers severe demand contraction across major Asian industrial markets.
Terrorist strikes in the US trigger global security overhaul and intense Middle Eastern geopolitical tensions.
Military intervention shifts production regimes and introduces sustained regional stability premiums.
Lehman Brothers collapse triggers systemic global liquidity crash and immediate crude demand destruction.
Regional uprisings across MENA networks disrupt production logistics and alter political risk baselines.
OPEC chooses market share defense over production cuts triggering a historic multi-year crude supply glut.
US withdraws from JCPOA cutting Iranian export access and tightening global sour crude availability.
Temporary collapse of Russia-Saudi compliance triggers record single-day production surges amidst COVID-19 demand drops.
Outbreak of war triggers structural shifts in European pipeline dependencies and massive international trade embargoes.
